questions.stem: I ______ the book yesterday. I ______ it for three days.
questions.explanation: 第一空后有明确过去时间状语yesterday，必须用一般过去时bought，排除B和D；第二空后有for three days表示持续到现在的时间段，必须用现在完成时have had，排除C。口诀：yesterday过去时，for/since完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: This is the school ______ I studied three years ago.
questions.explanation: 先行词是school（地点），定语从句I studied three years ago中不缺主语或宾语，缺的是地点状语（in the school），因此要用关系副词where，相当于in which。which/that在从句中需充当主语或宾语，who指人，均不符合。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The window ______ by the naughty boy just now.
questions.explanation: 主语window是break的承受者，需用被动语态（be+过去分词），排除A和C；时间状语just now（刚才）表示过去，因此用一般过去时的被动语态was broken。is broken是一般现在时被动，表示常态，不符合just now的时间要求。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —______ you ______ your homework yet? —Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 第一空后有yet（已经，用于疑问句），是现在完成时的标志词，用Have...finished；第二空后有ago（以前），是一般过去时的标志词，用finished。A选项第一空错误，C选项第二空错误，D选项两空都错误。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you don't know the word, you can ______ in the dictionary.
questions.explanation: look up表示'查阅（词典、资料）'，当代词作宾语时必须放在look和up中间，即look it up。look for表示'寻找'，不符合句意。B选项代词位置错误，C和D词义错误。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The news is ______. I feel very ______.
questions.explanation: exciting（令人兴奋的）修饰事物，excited（感到兴奋的）修饰人。第一空主语是news（消息），用exciting；第二空主语是I，用excited。口诀：-ing形容物，-ed形容人。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The old bridge is not strong enough to support heavy trucks. It may ______ if too many trucks cross it at the same time.
questions.explanation: 根据前文'不够坚固支撑卡车'可推测，如果太多卡车同时经过，桥可能会'倒塌'。collapse意为'倒塌、坍塌'，符合语境。stand（站立）、exist（存在）与文意相反，shake（摇晃）程度不够严重。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 根据下文'They often put food waste into the recyclable bin'（他们经常把厨余垃圾放进可回收垃圾桶）可知，学生们对使用哪个垃圾桶感到'困惑'。be confused about是固定搭配，意为'对……感到困惑'。worried（担心的）、excited（兴奋的）、surprised（惊讶的）均不符合语境逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a waste sorting (分类) program. As an environmental lover, I joined the volunteer team immediately. The first week was challenging. Many students were (8)______ about which bin to use. They often put food waste into the recyclable bin. To solve this problem, we made colorful posters and put them up near the bins. We also stood there during lunch time to (9)______ students. Slowly, things got better. More and more students began to understand the (10)______ of waste sorting. One day, I saw a younger student carefully reading the poster before throwing away his lunch box. He smiled at me and said, "I want to protect our (11)______." Now, after three weeks, the program has been very (12)______. The amount of waste has decreased by 30%. I feel proud that I can make a difference to our school life.

questions.explanation: 作为志愿者，我们在午餐时间站在垃圾桶旁是为了'指导'学生正确分类。guide意为'指导、引导'，符合语境。stop（阻止）、follow（跟随）、punish（惩罚）均不符合志愿者帮助学生的身份和上下文逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a waste sorting (分类) program. As an environmental lover, I joined the volunteer team immediately. The first week was challenging. Many students were (8)______ about which bin to use. They often put food waste into the recyclable bin. To solve this problem, we made colorful posters and put them up near the bins. We also stood there during lunch time to (9)______ students. Slowly, things got better. More and more students began to understand the (10)______ of waste sorting. One day, I saw a younger student carefully reading the poster before throwing away his lunch box. He smiled at me and said, "I want to protect our (11)______." Now, after three weeks, the program has been very (12)______. The amount of waste has decreased by 30%. I feel proud that I can make a difference to our school life.

questions.explanation: 根据上下文，学生们逐渐理解了垃圾分类的'重要性'。the importance of...意为'……的重要性'，是固定搭配。result（结果）、progress（进步）、difficulty（困难）代入后语义不通。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a waste sorting (分类) program. As an environmental lover, I joined the volunteer team immediately. The first week was challenging. Many students were (8)______ about which bin to use. They often put food waste into the recyclable bin. To solve this problem, we made colorful posters and put them up near the bins. We also stood there during lunch time to (9)______ students. Slowly, things got better. More and more students began to understand the (10)______ of waste sorting. One day, I saw a younger student carefully reading the poster before throwing away his lunch box. He smiled at me and said, "I want to protect our (11)______." Now, after three weeks, the program has been very (12)______. The amount of waste has decreased by 30%. I feel proud that I can make a difference to our school life.

questions.explanation: 文章主题是垃圾分类和环保，因此学生说想保护的是'环境'。environment（环境）与前文waste sorting和environmental lover呼应。school（学校）、family（家庭）、country（国家）范围过大或偏离主题。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a waste sorting (分类) program. As an environmental lover, I joined the volunteer team immediately. The first week was challenging. Many students were (8)______ about which bin to use. They often put food waste into the recyclable bin. To solve this problem, we made colorful posters and put them up near the bins. We also stood there during lunch time to (9)______ students. Slowly, things got better. More and more students began to understand the (10)______ of waste sorting. One day, I saw a younger student carefully reading the poster before throwing away his lunch box. He smiled at me and said, "I want to protect our (11)______." Now, after three weeks, the program has been very (12)______. The amount of waste has decreased by 30%. I feel proud that I can make a difference to our school life.

questions.explanation: 根据下文'垃圾量减少了30%'可知，这个项目非常'成功'。successful（成功的）符合语境。difficult（困难的）、boring（无聊的）、terrible（糟糕的）均与下文数据体现的积极结果矛盾。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a waste sorting (分类) program. As an environmental lover, I joined the volunteer team immediately. The first week was challenging. Many students were (8)______ about which bin to use. They often put food waste into the recyclable bin. To solve this problem, we made colorful posters and put them up near the bins. We also stood there during lunch time to (9)______ students. Slowly, things got better. More and more students began to understand the (10)______ of waste sorting. One day, I saw a younger student carefully reading the poster before throwing away his lunch box. He smiled at me and said, "I want to protect our (11)______." Now, after three weeks, the program has been very (12)______. The amount of waste has decreased by 30%. I feel proud that I can make a difference to our school life.

questions.stem: How many hours do teenagers spend on screens daily according to the study?
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第一段第二句'A new study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day'可知，青少年每天花大约7小时22分钟在屏幕上，约等于7小时。注意准确找到数字信息，不要误选D（22是分钟数）。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage: In recent years, "screen time" has become a hot topic among parents and teachers. A new study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs. However, not all screen time is harmful. Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, explains the difference between passive and active screen time. "Watching videos aimlessly is passive," he says. "But using educational apps or video chatting with family is active. It can actually help brain development." The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on passive screen time daily often have trouble sleeping. The blue light from screens affects the production of melatonin (褪黑激素), a chemical that helps us sleep. To solve this problem, experts suggest the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Schools in Shenzhen are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" reading hours, encouraging students to read paper books instead of e-books. "The key is balance," says Dr. Li. "Technology is not the enemy. We just need to use it wisely."

questions.stem: The underlined word "aimlessly" in Paragraph 2 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据前文'被动与主动屏幕时间的区别'，被动屏幕时间是指'无目的'地观看视频。aimlessly由aim（目的）+less（无）+ly（副词后缀）构成，意为'无目的地'，与without purpose同义。carefully（仔细地）、purposefully（有目的地）、quickly（快速地）均不符合构词法和上下文对比关系。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage: In recent years, "screen time" has become a hot topic among parents and teachers. A new study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs. However, not all screen time is harmful. Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, explains the difference between passive and active screen time. "Watching videos aimlessly is passive," he says. "But using educational apps or video chatting with family is active. It can actually help brain development." The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on passive screen time daily often have trouble sleeping. The blue light from screens affects the production of melatonin (褪黑激素), a chemical that helps us sleep. To solve this problem, experts suggest the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Schools in Shenzhen are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" reading hours, encouraging students to read paper books instead of e-books. "The key is balance," says Dr. Li. "Technology is not the enemy. We just need to use it wisely."

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. All screen time is bad for teenagers.
B. Schools should ban all electronic devices.
C. Active screen time may be beneficial.
D. Blue light helps produce melatonin.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第二段最后一句'It can actually help brain development'（它实际上可以帮助大脑发育）可推断，主动屏幕时间可能是有益的。A与第二段首句'not all screen time is harmful'矛盾；B与第四段学校采取的措施（screen-free hours而非ban all）不符；D与第三段'affects the production of melatonin'（影响褪黑激素分泌，且是负面影响）相反。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage: In recent years, "screen time" has become a hot topic among parents and teachers. A new study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs. However, not all screen time is harmful. Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, explains the difference between passive and active screen time. "Watching videos aimlessly is passive," he says. "But using educational apps or video chatting with family is active. It can actually help brain development." The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on passive screen time daily often have trouble sleeping. The blue light from screens affects the production of melatonin (褪黑激素), a chemical that helps us sleep. To solve this problem, experts suggest the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Schools in Shenzhen are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" reading hours, encouraging students to read paper books instead of e-books. "The key is balance," says Dr. Li. "Technology is not the enemy. We just need to use it wisely."

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: C. Teenagers and Screen Time: Finding Balance
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章首段提出屏幕时间问题，第二段区分主动与被动屏幕时间，第三段讲过度使用的危害，第四段讲解决方案，最后总结'The key is balance'。因此C项'青少年与屏幕时间：寻找平衡'最能概括全文。A只涉及手机历史，B只涉及海报制作（文中未提及），D只涉及睡眠（仅为细节）。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage: In recent years, "screen time" has become a hot topic among parents and teachers. A new study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs. However, not all screen time is harmful. Dr. Li, a scientist from Shenzhen University, explains the difference between passive and active screen time. "Watching videos aimlessly is passive," he says. "But using educational apps or video chatting with family is active. It can actually help brain development." The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on passive screen time daily often have trouble sleeping. The blue light from screens affects the production of melatonin (褪黑激素), a chemical that helps us sleep. To solve this problem, experts suggest the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Schools in Shenzhen are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" reading hours, encouraging students to read paper books instead of e-books. "The key is balance," says Dr. Li. "Technology is not the enemy. We just need to use it wisely."

options: A. Otherwise, you might feel too stressed.
B. Then, stick to your plan.
C. However, it's not as difficult as you think.
D. Or review your math notes during lunch break.
E. Without hobbies, life would be boring.
questions.explanation: 前文说'很多学生发现很难平衡学习和爱好'，此处需要转折承接，引出下文的建议。C项'然而，这并不像你想的那么难'起到承上启下的过渡作用，符合逻辑。A表示结果（否则），B表示顺承（然后），D是具体例子，E是原因，均不符合此处转折引出解决方案的语境。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. Here are some useful tips. (17)______ First, make a schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities. This helps you see where your time goes. (18)______ For example, if you love painting, set aside one hour every Saturday morning just for that. Second, use your free time wisely. Waiting for the bus? Listen to English tapes. (19)______ These small moments add up to big progress. Finally, don't forget to rest. Your brain needs breaks to work well. (20)______ Remember, balance is the key to a happy school life.

options: A. Otherwise, you might feel too stressed.
B. Then, stick to your plan.
C. However, it's not as difficult as you think.
D. Or review your math notes during lunch break.
E. Without hobbies, life would be boring.
questions.explanation: 前文讲'制定计划，写下所有任务'，此处应表示'然后坚持你的计划'。Then表示时间/逻辑上的顺承，stick to your plan与上文make a schedule呼应。A表转折警告，C表转折安慰，D是具体例子（且与下文For example重复），E表原因，均不符合此处'制定计划→执行计划'的逻辑顺序。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. Here are some useful tips. (17)______ First, make a schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities. This helps you see where your time goes. (18)______ For example, if you love painting, set aside one hour every Saturday morning just for that. Second, use your free time wisely. Waiting for the bus? Listen to English tapes. (19)______ These small moments add up to big progress. Finally, don't forget to rest. Your brain needs breaks to work well. (20)______ Remember, balance is the key to a happy school life.

options: A. Otherwise, you might feel too stressed.
B. Then, stick to your plan.
C. However, it's not as difficult as you think.
D. Or review your math notes during lunch break.
E. Without hobbies, life would be boring.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是'use your free time wisely'（明智利用碎片时间），前文举了'等公交车时听英语磁带'的例子，此处应平行列举另一个利用碎片时间的例子。D项'或者在午餐休息时复习数学笔记'符合语境，Or表示选择/并列关系，列举另一种利用碎片时间的方式。其他选项逻辑不符。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. Here are some useful tips. (17)______ First, make a schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities. This helps you see where your time goes. (18)______ For example, if you love painting, set aside one hour every Saturday morning just for that. Second, use your free time wisely. Waiting for the bus? Listen to English tapes. (19)______ These small moments add up to big progress. Finally, don't forget to rest. Your brain needs breaks to work well. (20)______ Remember, balance is the key to a happy school life.

options: A. Otherwise, you might feel too stressed.
B. Then, stick to your plan.
C. However, it's not as difficult as you think.
D. Or review your math notes during lunch break.
E. Without hobbies, life would be boring.
questions.explanation: 前文讲'don't forget to rest. Your brain needs breaks'（不要忘记休息，大脑需要休息），此处应说明不休息的后果。Otherwise（否则）引出反面结果，'你可能会感到压力太大'符合逻辑，强调休息的必要性。B表顺承，C表转折，D是例子，E是观点，均不符合此处'警告/后果'的语境。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. Here are some useful tips. (17)______ First, make a schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities. This helps you see where your time goes. (18)______ For example, if you love painting, set aside one hour every Saturday morning just for that. Second, use your free time wisely. Waiting for the bus? Listen to English tapes. (19)______ These small moments add up to big progress. Finally, don't forget to rest. Your brain needs breaks to work well. (20)______ Remember, balance is the key to a happy school life.
